articles.title: S&P 500 closes lower as tech stocks slide; SpaceX notches 3-day losing streak
articles.summary: S&P 500 fell 0.37% to 7,472.79, Nasdaq dropped 1.32% to 26,166.60, while Dow rose 148 points (+0.29%) to 51,712.71. Big Tech sold off sharply: GOOGL -5%, AMZN -4.8%, MSFT -3%, META -2.3%. Chips outperformed: MU +7%, SMCI +14%, INTC +5%. SpaceX fell 16.43% to $154.60 in third straight decline. Russell 2000 hit record close above 3,000.
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500: -0.37% → 7,472.79. Nasdaq Composite: -1.32% → 26,166.60. Dow Jones Industrial Average: +148.01 points (+0.29%) → 51,712.71. Major Tech: Alphabet (GOOGL) -5% on AI talent departures (John Jumper to Anthropic, Noam Shazeer to OpenAI). Amazon (AMZN) -4.8%. Meta Platforms (META) -2.3%. Microsoft (MSFT) -3%. Chip Outperformers: Micron Technology (MU) +~7% ahead of quarterly report Wednesday. AMD +>2%. Intel (INTC) +5%. Super Micro Computer (SMCI) +~14% on Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL4 platform announcement. SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $154.60, down 16.43% — worst day since June 12 IPO. IPO price $135; hit ~$225 intraday June 18; now ~25% off high. Caterpillar (CAT) +~4%. CVS Health (CVS) +4% on GLP-1 support program. Defense stocks slid: LMT -3.4%, LHX -3.2%, NOC -2.6%, GD -1.9%, RTX -1.6%. Apogee Therapeutics (APGE) +48% on AbbVie $10.9B acquisition offer.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Rises as Big Tech Stocks Weigh on Nasdaq After Weekend of U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
articles.summary: Major indexes mixed after weekend of tense U.S.-Iran peace talks. Dow +0.2%, S&P 500 -0.4%, Nasdaq -1.1%. Oil fell sharply after Treasury authorized Iranian oil sales for 60 days: WTI -2% to $74.50, Brent -4% to $77.50. 10Y Treasury yield 4.51%. Bitcoin ~$64,600. Gold -1% at $4,200/oz. DXY +0.2% to 101. April PCE hit nearly three-year high of 3.8% y/y. Fed officials open to raising rates this year.
articles.extracted_text: Major indexes mixed: Dow +0.2%, S&P 500 -0.4%, Nasdaq -1.1%. Follows two straight weeks of gains. Oil fell sharply after U.S. Treasury authorized sale of Iranian oil for 60 days: WTI -2% to $74.50/barrel, Brent -4% to $77.50/barrel. 10-year Treasury yield: 4.51% (up from 4.46% Thursday close). Bitcoin: ~$64,600. Gold futures: -1% at $4,200/oz. U.S. Dollar Index: +0.2% to 101. April PCE hit nearly three-year high of 3.8% y/y. Following last week's hawkish Fed meeting, officials signaled open to raising rates this year — median projection of quarter-point hike before 2027. Big Tech divergence: GOOGL -6%, AMZN -4% vs AAPL +0.5%, TSLA +2%. Micron (MU) jumped >6% to record high ahead of Wednesday earnings. Consensus: Q3 revenue ~$36.15 billion (~4x y/y), adjusted EPS $20.95 (vs $1.91). YTD shares quadrupled; up >850% over 12 months. SpaceX (SPCX) fell ~9% to ~$168 — lowest since IPO day June 12. Apogee Therapeutics (APGE) +47% on AbbVie $135.11/share ($10.9B) acquisition.

articles.title: Stock Market Today (June 22, 2026): Russell 2000 closes at 3,000 for the first time ever
articles.summary: Russell 2000 closed at record above 3,000 for first time. Nasdaq-100 quarterly rebalance added Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, Teradyne. S&P 500 added Marvell Technology and Flex. SMCI led S&P 500 gainers +14.68%. Definium Therapeutics +55.35%. Sleep Number -33% on bankruptcy. Chevron-MSFT 20-year deal for 2.7GW AI data center. Lucid cutting 18% workforce.
articles.extracted_text: Russell 2000 bucked trend, up 0.65% to record close above 3,000 — first time ever. Nasdaq-100 fell ~0.5% after quarterly rebalance. S&P 500 down 0.35% with 299 of 503 stocks advancing — breadth positive but mega-caps weighed. Nasdaq-100 additions (effective June 22): Astera Labs (replacing Charter), CoreWeave (Cognizant), Nebius Group (Insmed), Rocket Lab (Verisk), Teradyne (Zscaler). New members diverged: Rocket Lab -7.6%, CoreWeave -6.71% vs Astera Labs +3%. S&P 500 additions: Marvell Technology -1.89%, Flex +2.57%. Top gainers: Definium Therapeutics +55.35%, Super Micro Computer +14.68%, United Micro Electronics ADR +15%, Innio +11.9%, Apogee Therapeutics +47% premarket. Losers: Alamos Gold -13.72%, Gold Fields -10%, SpaceX -10.7%, Moderna -9.54%, Alphabet -7.04%, Sleep Number -33% (bankruptcy, $415M sale). Chevron-Microsoft 20-year deal: Chevron to supply 2.7GW data center campus in West Texas. Lucid Group (LCID) cutting 18% US workforce, COO out. Palantir (PLTR) -1.5% on France dropping Palantir AI tools. Bitcoin +1.23% to $64,894.32.

articles.title: Oil settles down more than 3% after US-Iran talks signal easing supply risks
articles.summary: Brent crude settled at $77.90/bbl (-3.31%), WTI at $74.82/bbl (-2.32%). US-Iran talks in Switzerland produced roadmap for final deal within 60 days. Treasury issued 60-day general license authorizing Iranian oil sales through Aug 21. ANZ estimates 2-3M bpd restored in first 4 weeks, but 1-2M bpd permanently lost. Full restoration unlikely this year.
articles.extracted_text: Brent crude settled at $77.90/bbl, down $2.67 (-3.31%). Intraday high: $82.30. WTI settled at $74.82/bbl, down $1.78 (-2.32%). WTI August contract: $73.86/bbl, down $1.99. High-level US-Iran talks concluded in Switzerland on Monday. Discussions began Sunday under memorandum of understanding extending April ceasefire for at least another 60 days. VP JD Vance: progress made, Strait of Hormuz open. US Treasury general license authorized Iranian oil and petrochemical sales through August 21. Iran did not negotiate nuclear program and accepted no new commitments. Tanker traffic resumed: two crude tankers carrying ~2 million barrels transited strait. Giovanni Staunovo (UBS): 'The release of those barrels is additional supply for the market.' Saudi Arabia's April crude exports fell to record low 3.99 million bpd. Iraq plans gradual restoration to 4.2-4.3 million bpd. ANZ supply recovery estimate: 2-3M bpd restored in first 4 weeks, additional 2-3.5M bpd potentially recoverable in Q3 2026 if stability holds, 1-2M bpd permanently or semi-permanently lost. 'Full restoration is unlikely this year.' US SPR: 9.05 million barrel draw last week — third-largest on record. Part of 172M barrel release agreement.

articles.title: Oil prices fall after U.S. authorizes Iranian crude sales
articles.summary: Brent fell 3.3% to $77.90, WTI lost 2.3% to $74.82. Treasury 60-day license authorizes production, delivery, sale of Iranian oil, including importation to US with dollar payments. Qatar and Pakistan confirmed roadmap for final deal within 60 days. David Roche (Quantum Strategy): apparent supply abundance reflects inventory liquidation, not production recovery.
articles.extracted_text: Brent crude futures fell 3.3% to close at $77.90 per barrel. WTI lost 2.3% to settle at $74.82 per barrel. VP JD Vance: 'great progress' in peace talks. Mediators Qatar and Pakistan: US and Iranian officials agreed on roadmap for final deal within 60 days. Treasury 60-day license: authorizes production, delivery and sale of Iranian oil, allows importation of Iranian crude to US, permits payment in dollars. Trump threatened renewed military action on Sunday. David Roche (Quantum Strategy): 'Middle East oil supply is currently close to prewar levels once crude held in storage and aboard tankers is included. However, the apparent abundance reflects inventory liquidation rather than a recovery in production, leaving the market vulnerable once those stockpiles are depleted.' Goldman Sachs: sustained supply shocks could accelerate shift toward EVs, eroding long-term crude demand.

articles.title: Alphabet paces for worst day in a year after AI talent exits
articles.summary: Alphabet shares slid 7% — worst day in a year. Two top AI researchers departed: Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead) to OpenAI, John Jumper (Nobel laureate, AlphaFold co-creator) to Anthropic. $225-270B market cap wipeout. Alphabet has raised $141B in debt and equity since October for AI infrastructure. MSFT CEO Nadella warned AI market is 'commoditized.'
articles.extracted_text: Alphabet shares slid 7% on Monday, on track for worst day in a year. Two top AI researchers announced departures: Noam Shazeer — VP of Engineering, co-lead of Google Gemini AI models — departed to OpenAI (announced Wednesday June 18). Returned to Google August 2024 after founding Character.AI; departure less than two years later. John Jumper — DeepMind VP and Engineering Fellow, Nobel Prize winner (2024), co-creator of AlphaFold — departed to Anthropic (announced Friday June 20). 9-year tenure at Google. Mounting concerns around commoditized AI. WSJ interview: Satya Nadella warned against 'AI Giants,' declared AI market commoditized. Alphabet raised $141 billion in debt and equity since October to fund AI infrastructure. $225-270B market cap wipeout. Additional negative sentiment from Gmail and YouTube outages same day.

articles.title: Super Micro Surges 17% on Nvidia Vera Rubin Partnership and Analyst Upgrade
articles.summary: SMCI surged 17% to $36.03 on 85M shares (1.7x average). Unveiled end-to-end DCBBS blueprint for Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL4 platform: up to 1,152 Rubin GPUs, 576 Vera CPUs, 3.2MW scalable to 1GW. GF Securities upgraded to Buy with $48 PT (55%+ upside). Short float 16.6%. Deployment scheduled H2 2026.
articles.extracted_text: SMCI surged 17% to $36.03 on volume of ~85M shares (1.7x 50-day average). Led all S&P 500 gainers. Short interest fell from 81.2M to 74.5M shares; short float 16.6% (days-to-cover 1.69). Vera Rubin NVL4 DCBBS blueprint: up to 1,152 Nvidia Rubin GPUs, 576 Nvidia Vera CPUs, Supermicro DLC-2 Direct Liquid Cooling supporting 362 kW per rack, Nvidia Quantum-X800 InfiniBand fabric, custom 52U rack enclosures, 3.2 megawatts of compute per unit scalable to 1 gigawatt. Deployment scheduled H2 2026. GF Securities upgrade: Hold → Buy, price target $48 (55%+ upside). 'The roughly 28% decline following a $7 billion equity offering earlier this month had created an attractive entry point, and completing the capital raise actually removes a key overhang.' First bullish rating change in months. 52-week high $62.36, 52-week low $19.48. Market cap ~$23.3 billion.

articles.title: SpaceX stock falls 15%, continuing selloff after blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) fell 16.13% to $155.16 — third straight decline, worst day since June 12 IPO. Still up 37% from $135 IPO price but ~25% below record $201 close. Cash: $100.8B as of June 19. Q1 2026 net loss: $4.28B. Announced senior unsecured notes offering. Average post-IPO buyer has seen nearly all gains wiped out.
articles.extracted_text: Monday's decline: ~15% (actual: -16.13% at 3:47 PM EDT, last price $155.16). Third consecutive day of losses. Previous week: -5% Wednesday, -3.6% Thursday (market closed Friday for Juneteenth). IPO price: $135 per share (began trading June 12, 2026). Initial surge pushed market cap above Amazon, briefly above Microsoft on Tuesday June 16. Still up 37% from IPO price as of Thursday close pre-holiday. Cash & equivalents as of June 19: $100.8 billion. 2025 net loss: $4.9 billion. Q1 2026 net loss: $4.28 billion. Elon Musk became world's first trillionaire. Thousands of new millionaires minted. Average post-IPO buyer: nearly all early gains wiped out by end of last week. Monday announced senior unsecured notes offering.

articles.title: SpaceX Stock Plunges to Lowest Price Since IPO Day
articles.summary: SpaceX shares tumbled to lowest price since IPO day, down more than 10% to $166. First bond sale announced Monday to repay borrowings under $20 billion bridge loan taken for xAI merger. Experts caution continued volatility from small float; potential updraft from index inclusion.
articles.extracted_text: SpaceX (SPCX) shares tumbled on Monday to their lowest price since IPO day. Down more than 10% at $166, near their first-day close. First bond sale announced Monday (size not disclosed) to repay borrowings under a $20 billion bridge loan taken for the xAI merger. Experts caution continued volatility from small float; potential updraft from index inclusion (some expected as early as today), while future selling pressure could come as lock-up periods expire.

articles.title: Treasury Yield Curve Today: June 22, 2026 — Rates & Spread Monitor
articles.summary: Treasury yields rose across the curve. 10Y at 4.52%, up 0.06pp from previous session. 2Y at 4.04%. 10Y-2Y spread at +0.27 (FRED data, curve no longer inverted). 1Y bill at 4.136% (highest since Aug 1, 2025). Markets pricing potential rate hike as early as September.
articles.extracted_text: US 10 Year Note Bond Yield rose to 4.52% on June 22, 2026, marking a 0.06 percentage points increase from the previous session. Over the past month, yield edged up by 0.02 points and is 0.17 points higher than a year ago. 10Y-2Y spread: +0.27 (FRED data, updated June 22, 2026 4:02 PM CDT). TradingEconomics: 9 of 19 Fed policymakers now project at least one rate increase before year-end, with markets increasingly pricing in a potential hike as early as September. Higher rate expectations continued to provide support for Treasury yields. PCE price index release this week — Fed's preferred measure of inflation.

articles.title: Micron Earnings Preview: Profit Growth Approaching 1,000%
articles.summary: Micron (MU) up 4-7% ahead of Wednesday's Q3 FY2026 earnings. Consensus: revenue $34.98-36.15B (~4x y/y), adj EPS $19.15-20.95. Price targets: Bernstein $1,300, Needham $1,550. Stock up >850% over 12 months. Global memory shortage persists. Morgan Stanley expects results to top consensus.
articles.extracted_text: Micron Technology (MU) jumped 4-7% on Monday ahead of quarterly earnings Wednesday after the bell. Consensus estimates: Q3 revenue ~$34.98-36.15 billion (~4x from a year ago), adjusted EPS $19.15-20.95 (vs. $1.91 last year). YTD: shares quadrupled; up >850% over 12 months. Morgan Stanley: expectations rising; global memory shortage persists; results seen topping consensus. Price-target hikes: $1,300 (Bernstein), $1,550 (Needham), $1,250 (Wolfe Research), $900 (Goldman Sachs). Zacks estimates Q3 sales surged 276% to $34.98 billion. Company expects adjusted EPS of $19.15 at midpoint of guidance range.

articles.title: Fed Hawkishness Spurs Rotation into Value and Industrials
articles.summary: June 22 marked a decisive day of sector rotation as investors moved away from high-flying tech growth stocks into economically sensitive sectors and value names. The pattern is consistent: AI infrastructure gets sold, cash recycles into non-tech sectors and small caps, Dow makes new highs while Nasdaq treads water or drops.
articles.extracted_text: June 22, 2026 marked a decisive day of sector rotation in the stock market, as investors moved away from high-flying technology growth stocks and into more economically sensitive sectors and value-oriented names. The pattern is consistent: AI infrastructure gets sold, the cash recycles into non-tech sectors and small caps, and the Dow makes new highs while the Nasdaq treads water or drops. Fed hawkishness from last week's meeting — with 9 of 19 policymakers projecting at least one rate increase before year-end — accelerated the rotation out of duration-sensitive growth names.

articles.title: AbbVie to Acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 Billion
articles.summary: AbbVie agreed to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $135.11/share — 50% premium — valuing company at ~$10.9 billion. APGE surged 47% to $133. ABBV up ~5%. CEO cited 'mega-blockbuster peak sales potential' for atopic dermatitis and asthma pipeline assets.
articles.extracted_text: AbbVie (ABBV) agreed to buy Apogee Therapeutics (APGE) for $135.11/share — a 50% premium — valuing the company at ~$10.9 billion. CEO statement: 'Apogee's pipeline adds highly differentiated clinical-stage assets … including atopic dermatitis and asthma … mega-blockbuster peak sales potential.' APGE shares surged 47% to $133; ABBV up ~5%.

articles.title: DXY US Dollar Index Rises as Hawkish Fed Supports Greenback
articles.summary: DXY rose to 101.02, up 0.17% on the session. Over the past month, USD has strengthened 1.79%, and is up 2.64% over 12 months. Hawkish Fed rate expectations supporting the greenback. Markets pricing 40-45 bps of hikes.
articles.extracted_text: The DXY exchange rate rose to 101.0168 on June 22, 2026, up 0.17% from the previous session. Over the past month, the United States Dollar has strengthened 1.79%, and is up by 2.64% over the last 12 months. EUR/USD: markets now pricing some 40-45 basis points of hikes.

articles.title: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread
articles.summary: ICE BofA US High Yield OAS at 2.66% as of June 19 close. IG credit spreads near 25-year tights at ~80 bps. Tight credit spreads indicate no stress in corporate bond market despite equity tech selloff.
articles.extracted_text: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread: 2.66% as of June 19, 2026. IG OAS at approximately 80 basis points, near 25-year tights. BBB bonds trade at roughly 100 bps, AA at 51 bps, AAA at approximately 40 bps. These levels are significantly tighter than the long-term average of roughly 150 bps.

articles.title: Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan Dies at 100
articles.summary: Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan (1926-2026) died at age 100. Served 1987-2006, known as 'The Maestro,' presided over 'Great Moderation' era. Fed statement praised his leadership achieving sustained price stability. Historical context, limited market impact.
articles.extracted_text: Former Fed chair Alan Greenspan (1926-2026) died at age 100. Known as 'The Maestro,' he presided over the 'Great Moderation' era of low inflation and growth. Served 1987-2006. Critics link his deregulatory philosophy to the later financial crisis. Fed statement: 'Under his leadership, the Federal Reserve achieved a sustained era of price stability that supported economic growth and helped anchor the public's confidence in the independent central bank.'

articles.title: Russell Reconstitution June 2026: Larger Leaders, Stronger Small Caps
articles.summary: Russell 2000 total market cap increased from $2.7 trillion at 2025 reconstitution to $3.5 trillion in 2026. Stronger small cap fundamentals supporting the index's record run above 3,000.
articles.extracted_text: The total market capitalization of the Russell 2000 increased from $2.7 trillion at the 2025 reconstitution to $3.5 trillion in 2026. Larger leaders, stronger small caps. Semi-annual reconstitution schedule doubles operational friction.

articles.title: Stock Market News June 22, 2026: Dow posts back-to-back gains, S&P 500 falls and Nasdaq ends sharply lower
articles.summary: Dow posted back-to-back gains while S&P 500 fell and Nasdaq ended sharply lower. US crude oil settled below $74 a barrel. Key reads: flood of oil to hit markets, bull market not ending on Fed rate hikes, SpaceX frenzy cooling, Micron earnings a must-watch event.
articles.extracted_text: Dow posts back-to-back gains, S&P 500 falls and Nasdaq ends sharply lower even as U.S. crude oil settles below $74 a barrel. Top reads: flood of oil set to hit energy markets, bull market not ending on Fed rate hikes, initial SpaceX frenzy cooling off, Micron earnings a must-watch event with profit growth approaching 1,000%.
